message:
Support multiple Linear workspaces via custom env vars (#4)

## Problem

streamlinear hardcoded `LINEAR_API_TOKEN` for authentication, making it
impossible to use multiple MCP instances for different Linear workspaces
(e.g., personal and work accounts).

When users configured two MCP entries with different env vars like:
- linear-personal: LINEAR_API_TOKEN
- linear-work: LINEAR_WORK_API_TOKEN

The linear-work instance would fail because streamlinear ignored
LINEAR_WORK_API_TOKEN and only looked for LINEAR_API_TOKEN.

## Solution

Added automatic detection of any environment variable matching the pattern
`LINEAR*_API_TOKEN`. This allows users to name their tokens descriptively:
- LINEAR_API_TOKEN (default, highest priority)
- LINEAR_WORK_API_TOKEN
- LINEAR_PERSONAL_API_TOKEN
- etc.

If multiple tokens are present, LINEAR_API_TOKEN takes precedence for
backward compatibility.

## Changes

- linear-core.ts: Added getTokenFromEnv() to scan for LINEAR*_API_TOKEN
- cli.ts: Updated resolveToken() with same logic
- index.ts: Updated error message
- README.md: Added multi-workspace setup documentation
- package.json: Removed unnecessary shebang from build scripts

## Testing

Verified both tokens work:
- LINEAR_API_TOKEN → Jesse/FSCK workspace ✓
- LINEAR_WORK_API_TOKEN → Prime Radiant workspace ✓

Co-authored-by: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
